news.title: Tight housing market boosts New York office conversions
news.para: New York real estate firms are increasingly converting office buildings into apartment complexes as an antidote to the city's housing crunch, tapping into tax incentives also designed to increase the supply of affordable homes. "My belief is just about any building could be converted as residential,"during a tour of an office highrise at 77 Water Street the firm is converting into a residential building.

news.title: ‘No power of review’: Bombay HC nixes deemed conveyance to housing society
news.para: The Bombay High Court has quashed a deemed conveyance order, stating the competent authority lacked the power to entertain a second application on a finalized issue. Justice Farhan Dubash ruled the deputy registrar exercised an unrecognized review power, barring the second application by principles of finality and res judicata.

news.title: Odisha government to introduce new act soon as it plans major shift in urban rental housing
news.para: Odisha is set to introduce the draft Odisha Urban Area Rent Control Act, 2026, replacing the 1967 act. The legislation mandates written agreements, caps security deposits, and establishes rent authorities and courts for speedy dispute resolution, aiming to balance tenant and landlord rights.

news.title: Panchkula civic body to discuss property tax waiver in its first General House meeting
news.para: Panchkula's first General House meeting on June 16 will focus on key development agendas, including making Ward No. 4 a model cleanliness ward and implementing a property tax amnesty to recover ₹100 crore in dues. The meeting will also address civic infrastructure upgrades, waterlogging solutions, and broader development goals like recreation centers and sports facilities.

news.title: Gurugram: DTCP to resume crackdown on illegal builds in DLF phases next week
news.para: The Department of Town and Country Planning (DTCP) in Gurugram is set to resume large-scale enforcement against illegal constructions in DLF Phase 3 and other phases. Following a high court order, over 3,500 properties are now eligible for action, targeting extensions, stilt parking violations, and unauthorized commercial use.

news.title: IT hubs, Aerocity, Metro rail network feature in revised Visakhapatnam's master plan
news.para: Visakhapatnam Metropolitan Region Development Authority submitted a revised master plan-2041 to the Andhra Pradesh government, prioritizing sustainable growth with infrastructure, economic development, and environmental conservation. The plan, designed for an estimated population of 66.3 lakh by 2041, balances land use, transportation, and industrial growth across three districts.

news.title: E-registration only for a few categories, Uttar Pradesh says after strike by Noida deed writers
news.para: Deed writers' strike over e-registration fears in Greater Noida has prompted a clarification from the registration department. The online system will only apply to a limited category of government and semi-government property transactions, not all sale deeds. This aims to reassure professionals concerned about job security, with private property deals remaining under the traditional process.

news.title: Noida: In Golf View II people are walking with helmets on
news.para: Residents in Noida's Golf View II are now wearing helmets for daily activities like walking and cycling due to falling plaster and concrete from buildings. This extreme measure follows a recent fatality in Greater Noida West where a biker died from falling debris, heightening safety concerns across housing societies.

news.title: Gurugram civic body to divide city into eight zones under two clusters
news.para: Gurugram Municipal Corporation has reorganized its administrative structure, dividing the city into eight zones under two clusters to enhance governance and service delivery. This move aims to accelerate decision-making, increase accountability, and provide more efficient, transparent, and timely services to residents.

news.title: Airport, New Noida effect: Why Bulandshahr land prices are rising
news.para: Bulandshahr landowners anticipate a windfall as the proposed Dadri-Noida-Ghaziabad Investment Region (New Noida) expands the industrial network.  With land acquisition rates significantly lower than market prices, residents are demanding higher compensation. The project, part of the Delhi-Mumbai Industrial Corridor, aims for a massive industrial expansion by 2041.

news.title: Delhi: NDMC offers 10% rebate on property tax payments made by June 30
news.para: New Delhi Municipal Council announces a 10 percent property tax rebate for the financial year 2026-27. Property owners can avail this concession by paying their dues on or before June 30. This initiative encourages early compliance and aims to boost revenue collection. Taxpayers can make payments online. The NDMC urges timely deposits to avoid last-minute rush and secure the discount.

news.title: Prayagraj: PDA reviews 10-year property notices for regularisation under the relaxed provisions
news.para: The Prayagraj Development Authority is reviewing over 30,000 building violation notices from the past decade. This initiative aims to regularize properties under the relaxed provisions of Master Plan-2031, offering owners a chance to legalize constructions by paying compounding fees. The exercise is expected to boost PDA's finances by an estimated Rs 1,500 crore.

news.title: Chennai civic body seeks direction to tie property defaulters under loan recovery
news.para: Greater Chennai Corporation is seeking CERSAI's intervention to compel banks to recover pending property tax dues from loan defaulters. The civic body aims to link property tax defaults with CIBIL scores and urges banks to deduct outstanding amounts during loan recovery. This initiative follows a property tax collection rate of 80% last fiscal year.

news.title: Kerala: Legal hurdles delay Infopark Phase III AI township development
news.para: India's first integrated AI township, Infopark Phase III, is stalled. Jurisdictional complications hinder the land pooling model. Authorities seek state government intervention to amend rules and proceed. The project aims for carbon-negative, water-positive, and zero-waste status. Infopark also plans to lease 20 acres for development and infrastructure upgrades.

news.title: Kolkata: NKDA starts door-to-door property tax, trade licence survey
news.para: NKDA has launched a door-to-door survey in New Town to physically verify property tax self-assessments and trade licences for residential and commercial buildings. This initiative aims to update NKDA's data for accurate tax assessment and to identify and curb unauthorized conversion of residential properties into commercial spaces.

news.title: Panaji: Karapur villagers give June 15 deadline to halt The House of Abhinandan Lodha’s housing project
news.para: Villagers protesting The House of Abhinandan Lodha's luxury housing project in Karapur-Sarvan marched to Panaji, demanding a stop-work order. They allege the project lacks environmental clearance and other approvals, with activists citing regulatory violations and environmental damage. Officials assured the matter would be reviewed.

news.title: Hyderabad: Medipally HMDA layout owners cry foul over prohibitory list
news.para: Plot owners in a Medipally HMDA layout are facing issues with their plots being on a prohibitory list and a lack of amenities three years after purchase. Despite HMDA preparing for further auctions, unresolved legal disputes, encroachments, and a missing approach road are causing significant hardship for buyers.

news.title: Cycle of people encroaching and government giving homes can’t go on forever: Maharashtra CM
news.para: Maharashtra CM Devendra Fadnavis defended the slum demolition drive, citing court orders and the need to end the cycle of encroachment. He also highlighted significant foreign investment in data centers in Maharashtra and praised PM Modi's transformative leadership and welfare schemes.

news.title: Homes under construction may get costlier as Ahmedabad developers weigh price hike
news.para: Ahmedabad real estate developers will meet on June 15 to discuss potential price increases for under-construction properties. Escalating construction input costs, driven by West Asia conflict-related supply disruptions, are prompting developers to reassess pricing strategies for projects initiated in the last six months.
